frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: pupil size as a behavioral marker of central arousal state

frameworks.name: neuromodulatory control of arousal
frameworks.one_line: serotonergic and noradrenergic systems jointly shape brainstem arousal control

frameworks.name: locus coeruleus noradrenergic signaling
frameworks.one_line: challenges assumption that pupil dynamics reflect LC noradrenaline alone

core_question: How do serotonergic and noradrenergic brainstem systems jointly and uniquely shape pupil-linked arousal in awake animals?
key_assumptions: pupil size indexes central arousal state
dorsal raphe and locus coeruleus contribute to brainstem control of waking
neuromodulatory systems can be simultaneously accessed in awake mice
manipulations: simultaneous recording/perturbation of serotonergic dorsal raphe and noradrenergic locus coeruleus
dissociating serotonergic effects on pupil with vs without locus coeruleus involvement
key_findings: serotonergic and noradrenergic activity co-fluctuated in awake mice
serotonergic dorsal raphe neurons influenced pupil size partly via noradrenergic locus coeruleus populations
a component of serotonergic control of pupil dynamics was independent of locus coeruleus
interpretations: pupil dynamics reflect interplay between distinct neurochemical systems, not noradrenaline alone
challenges the common one-to-one mapping between pupil size and locus coeruleus noradrenergic activity
